lesson.menu_title: Bad News First -- Explaining a Delay
lesson.menu_desc: Give bad news in three moves: cause, impact, recovery plan -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Bad News First -- Explaining a Delay
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Diogo Leal | Aula 9 -- Bad News First

plan_row.tema: Bad News First -- Explaining a Delay
plan_row.foco: Cause and result (because / so / that is why) + be going to
plan_row.atividade: Bad-news call in three moves + angry client role-play

bank.label: The five lines of a bad-news call
items: I want to give you a heads-up: the migration is delayed.
The root cause is the license approval.
The impact is two weeks on the go-live.
I apologize for the delay, and here is my plan.
We are going to reschedule the go-live to July 5th.
I will send you the new plan by Friday.
The vendor cancelled your test environment. You lose four days.
Two developers moved to another project. The tests are now three weeks of work, not one.
The client changed the requirements in the middle of the sprint. The go-live cannot happen in June.

items.situation: Your client asks: 'Why am I hearing this from your colleague and not from you?'
tips: Own it: 'You are right, and I apologize.'
Then go straight to the facts.

items.situation: Give the root cause of the delay in one sentence.
The client asks: 'How does this affect my date?'

tips: Then move to the plan. Do not repeat it.

items.situation: Your manager wants a recovery plan, right now.

tips: Promise one thing, with a date.
'I will send the new plan by Friday.'

pressure.title: Answer live
items: Why should I trust the new date if you missed the first one?
Who is responsible for this delay?
What do I tell my own boss this afternoon?

pressure.text: Answer first, reason second, plan third. Never start with the excuse.

listenings.text: Diogo, we have a problem. The client called me this morning. They heard that the migration is late, and they heard it from someone else, not from you. That is the worst way to get bad news. Please call them today. Tell them what happened, tell them what it means for their date, and tell them your plan. Do not apologize five times. Apologize once, and move to the plan. And give them a new date that you can actually keep.

Thank you for taking the call. I want to be direct with you. The migration is delayed by two weeks. Here is what happened. We needed a license approval, and it arrived nine days late. That is the root cause. Here is the impact. The go-live moves from June 20th to July 5th. Nothing else in the plan changes. I apologize for the delay, and I also apologize for telling you late. Here is my recovery plan. We are going to test in parallel, and we are going to add one weekend run. If that works, we recover three days. I will send you the new plan by Friday, and I will call you every Monday until we go live.

extra_audio.text: Diogo gives Sophia a heads-up before the client call. He explains the root cause: the license arrived nine days late. Sophia asks about the impact on the client. Diogo gives the new go-live date. Finally, he presents the recovery plan: parallel tests and a weekend run.
